item.title: The job market is much stronger than economists expected. Why?
item.description: Americans say it's tough to find a job, but employers just added a surprisingly strong 172,000 new hires in May.

item.title: Adviser to Iran's supreme leader says "ball is in Trump's court"
item.description: It remains unclear how close the U.S. and Iran are to striking a deal to end the Iran war amid continued hostilities between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah.

item.title: Graham Platner faces another controversy days ahead of Maine Senate primary
item.description: This week, the New York Times reported allegations of Platner's "unsettling" behavior toward women he dated, including one claim that he was physically abusive, which Platner denies.

item.title: Ned Jarrett, NASCAR Hall of Famer and CBS announcer, dies at 93
item.description: Ned Jarrett was inducted into the NASCAR Hall of Fame in 2011 after 50 career wins on the sport's top circuit.

item.title: DOJ lawyers could face discipline over handling of transgender care probe
item.description: U.S. District Judge Mary McElroy had lambasted Justice Department lawyers in a decision last month and accused them of misrepresenting and withholding information.

item.title: Putin sees "no point" in meeting with Zelenskyy to discuss ending war
item.description: President Zelenskyy chided Putin in his first public message to the Russian leader, who called it "boorish" on Friday.

item.title: Trump says he wants Pulte to "start the process" of shrinking intel office
item.description: President Trump told the Wall Street Journal he may even want to terminate the Office of the Director of National Intelligence altogether.

item.title: Employers added 172,000 jobs in May, surging past expectations
item.description: The labor market continues to show strength despite rising inflation and concerns about slowing economic growth.

item.title: Flesh-eating New World screwworm fly is not a food safety or virus issue, Texas officials say
item.description: Texas Gov. Greg Abbott and other officials provided an update on the New World screwworm fly after the parasite was discovered in a 3-week-old calf in Zavala County, Texas. Officials say food safety is not at risk and that the parasite is not transmissible like a virus.

item.title: Here are 3 places to earn 4% on your money starting this June
item.description: Want to boost your savings with a high-rate account right now? Here are three ways you can do that this month.

item.title: Meet the Belmont Stakes horses running in the 2026 race
item.description: The Belmont Stakes will host a New York rematch of the top two finishing horses from the Kentucky Derby​ to wrap up horse racing's Triple Crown for 2026.

item.title: Judge orders detention of ex-CIA official found with $40 million in gold bars
item.description: Former CIA official David Rush was arrested in May after FBI agents found gold bars worth about $40 million at his home while probing whether he had lied about his educational and military background, according to court records.

item.title: Disturbing video shows man attacking 21-year-old over lake access
item.description: Steven Dana, 70, is facing multiple charges, including attempted murder, after a video emerged of him attacking a 21-year-old man who was riding a jet ski in Massachusetts' Lake Maspenock with friends. CBS News Boston's Anna Meiler reports.

item.title: CENTCOM disputes claims that Iran fired warning missiles, drones at U.S. warships
item.description: The U.S. military is disputing Iran's claims that it fired missiles and drones to warn U.S. warships to retreat. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata reports.

item.title: Journalist in Iran's Evin Prison pleads for medical help for U.S. captives
item.description: CBS News has obtained a voice memo recorded by Iranian American journalist Reza Valizadeh, who has been detained in Evin Prison for over a year.

item.title: Space station crew briefly moves to "safe haven" amid concerns over leaks
item.description: Out of an abundance of caution, NASA briefly directed five of the seven crew members aboard the International Space Station to wait inside the docked SpaceX Crew Dragon "Freedom" spacecraft.

item.title: Meet "The Baklava Guy" sweetening up the Knicks games in NYC
item.description: Jacob Komarow, known as Roy Donk or "The Baklava Guy" giving out sweet treats outside Knicks games in New York City, joins CBS News with more on his mission to unite basketball fans with the popular pastry.

item.title: 3 homebuying advantages to consider this June
item.description: Mortgage interest rates may not be ideal, but there are still advantages to buying a home this June. Here are three.

item.title: Judge blocks Trump policies that halted many legal immigration cases
item.description: A federal judge blocked a series of measures that have prevented officials from granting asylum, green cards and other legal immigration benefits to many immigrants.

item.title: Remembering the legendary 1973 Belmont Stakes when Secretariat won the Triple Crown
item.description: The 2026 Belmont Stakes are taking place on Saturday in New York. CBS News' Jon LaPook recalls the legendary 1973 Belmont Stakes, where Secretariat took the Triple Crown and made sports history.

item.title: $70,000 CD vs. $70,000 high-yield savings account vs. $70,000 money market account: Which will earn more now?
item.description: Interest returns with all three account types will be similar, but they won't be identical. Here's what to know now.

item.title: Anthony Head, "Buffy the Vampire Slayer" and "Ted Lasso" star, dies at 72
item.description: Anthony Head played librarian and mentor Rupert Giles in "Buffy the Vampire Slayer" and recently appeared in "Ted Lasso."

item.title: Senate fails to extend key surveillance program as deadline nears
item.description: A procedural vote failed in the Senate early Friday, and a provision of the spy powers law is set to expire June 12.

item.title: Trump accuses Democrats of rigging California primaries
item.description: President Trump is alleging that Democrats are "rigging" the California elections as votes are still being counted for some of the state's primary races. CBS News' Fin Gómez reports.

item.title: Mom of American missing in Japan says they argued over ChatGPT
item.description: James "Weston" Higginbotham went missing one week ago while on a family vacation in Japan.

item.title: Tom Homan, Trump's border czar, reacts to abuse allegations at Delaney Hall ICE facility
item.description: Tom Homan, President Trump's border czar, is reacting to prolonged protests outside New Jersey's Delaney Hall ICE facility amid allegations of inhumane conditions for detained migrants. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez reports.

item.title: Second Facebook privacy settlement payment is coming soon
item.description: The additional payouts come from uncashed settlement funds and will be issued to eligible claimants beginning on June 9.

item.title: 1 dead, 3 hurt in high school graduation shooting in Northern California
item.description: One person died and three others, including a child, were injured in a shooting Wednesday night at a high school graduation in Northern California, officials said.

item.title: Who's really at your door? App delivery drivers in California found using accounts that aren't theirs
item.description: Do you know who you're opening your door to? Lawmakers respond after a CBS California consumer investigation found food delivery drivers using rented or stolen app accounts to bypass background checks, exposing a loophole that could put customers at risk.

item.title: The Iranian Revolution, in black-and-white
item.description: "Persepolis" depicts a young girl's coming of age amidst turmoil in Tehran.
